steps.title: Identify the structures on each side
steps.content: Left side: An incomplete independent clause—”The pedagogy...is rooted in several central ___.” Right side: A prepositional phrase, followed by a relative clause (“who sought to parallel the linguistic learning environment”), followed by an independent clause with a new subject (“the method emphasizes...”).

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: A comma cannot separate two independent clauses; a period or semicolon is required. Choice C uses a period after “tenets,” which correctly creates a sentence boundary, allowing the second sentence to open with “Developed by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki”—a participial phrase that precedes the relative clause and main clause in proper order. Choices A and B preserve both independent clauses in a single sentence, violating the rule; choice D is ungrammatical. That matches choice C — C is correct.
